html:
Horizontal camera translation
All camera centers lie on one straight horizontal line; each red arrow points to the next position.
⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: Sequence of perspective camera frustums drawn as 2D black wireframe outlines, aligned in a row along the Z axis
Each camera frustum has a simple outlined isosceles triangle with a vertex at the pinhole (center)
Solid black dot indicating each camera's optical center (pinhole)
A single thick red line segment between adjacent optical centers, connecting them rather than passing through the frustums
Plain white background, no volume cues, shadows, or 3D lighting
No background grid or axes

interactions.label: Cameras
interactions.teaches: Shows more or fewer discrete camera positions, clarifying whether the viewer perceives a jump or a smooth sequence of forward motion.

interactions.label: Step Distance
interactions.teaches: Demonstrates how much the camera moves between each snapshot, affecting how far apart the arrows and cameras appear.

demo_steps.title: Start and End Camera
demo_steps.narration: Let's start with just two cameras: the black frustums at the beginning and after a forward motion along the Z axis. Notice the thick red arrow connecting their centers, showing a single step of translation.

demo_steps.title: Visualizing Continuous Motion
demo_steps.narration: Increasing to three cameras reveals the intervening position. Now, there are two red arrows: each represents a forward step, making it clearer that the camera moves repeatedly in the same direction and spacing.

demo_steps.title: Stepping Further Forward
demo_steps.narration: Widening the spacing between cameras stretches the red arrows. Each camera now covers more ground per step, illustrating a faster or more dramatic forward motion toward the scene.

demo_steps.title: Smooth Camera Path
demo_steps.narration: With six closely spaced cameras, the forward translation looks almost continuous. Multiple short red arrows between nearby optical centers show how a moving camera samples its path in small increments.

plan.camera_suggestion: Use an orthographic top view (looking straight down the Y axis), so the camera frustums and connecting arrows form a clear, flat diagram across the Z axis. Frame all cameras and arrows horizontally within view.
plan.notes: Draw camera frustums as open black wireframe triangles, matching the textbook flat sketch style. Connect only the optical centers with thick red lines as in the original; never have arrows pass through frustum volumes. Avoid perspective distortion, shading, or any 3D volume cues. Keep background pure white and all elements crisp. No axes should be visible.

elements: Sequence of five perspective cameras (frustums) aligned horizontally
Each camera represented by a frustum with black lines and a black center point (pinhole)
Thick red arrows pointing right, entering each camera's optical center and exiting the frustum forward
White background

interactions.label: Camera Spacing
interactions.teaches: Adjusts the distance between each camera along the forward (Z) axis, showing the continuity of forward motion.

interactions.label: Arrow Length
interactions.teaches: Controls the length of the motion arrows to represent the 'amount' of forward translation per camera step.

interactions.label: Number of Cameras
interactions.teaches: Demonstrates the path of the camera through more or fewer discrete positions during its forward motion.

demo_steps.title: Single Forward Motion
demo_steps.narration: Let's start with just two cameras: one at the start and one after moving forward. Notice the red arrow that bridges the gap, showing the direction and distance of motion along the camera's optical (Z) axis.

demo_steps.title: Adding More Steps
demo_steps.narration: Now, see what happens as we increase the number of camera snapshots along the path. Each camera is displaced forward, and a red arrow indicates the motion from one to the next. This sequence builds up a sense of continuous forward translation.

demo_steps.title: Changing Distance and Speed
demo_steps.narration: You can adjust how far the camera moves each step or how many steps are shown. Notice that increasing the spacing or the arrow length exaggerates the translation, while more steps make the motion appear smoother and more continuous—just like a camera driving forward toward a scene.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Start with a front-facing, slightly elevated orthographic view to clearly see the aligned sequence of frustums and red arrows. Zoom so all cameras and arrows are visible horizontally.
interactionPlan.notes: Use transparent frustum geometry for each camera with bold black edge lines and a filled black sphere at each optical center. Red arrows should be visually striking and parallel to the Z axis (scene forward direction). Cameras should not overlap even when spacing is low. Keep background clean/white for clarity.

attempts.html:
Forward camera motion
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Red arrows in the generated figure penetrate all camera frustums, while in the source image arrows always point from the left and stop at the camera pinhole.
Each camera in the generated figure has its own motion arrow, while the source figure has a single continuous arrow pointing from one camera to the next.
The frustum volumes in the generated figure are outlined with extra 3D edges and a faint filled face not seen in the source figure.
The vertical center line on the image plane is present in the generated figure but does not exist in the source figure.
Camera frustum shapes in the rendered screenshot are much more 3D and shaded than the plain flat 2D sketch style of the source image.
evaluation.notes: Motion arrows are incorrect; camera frustums too 3D and do not match the simple 2D sketch; UI is rich but the figure misrepresents the core concept.
action_items: Redesign arrows so there is a single thick red arrow segment between each camera as in the source (not passing through cameras).
Remove the image plane vertical center line and the semi-transparent frustum volume fill—match the original simple black wireframe sketch style.
Flatten the rendering so frustums look like plain 2D outlined shapes; avoid unnecessary 3D effects.
Remove 3D volume cues from the camera bodies to match the 2D appearance.
Reconsider the use of animation/steps to focus on transitions that match textbook intent.

feedback.rationale: The critic cites concept misunderstanding and wrong primitives, with action items requiring a fundamental redesign of the arrow and frustum representations to match the 2D source figure.
action_items: Redesign arrows so there is a single thick red arrow segment between each camera as in the source (not passing through cameras).
Remove the image plane vertical center line and the semi-transparent frustum volume fill—match the original simple black wireframe sketch style.
Flatten the rendering so frustums look like plain 2D outlined shapes; avoid unnecessary 3D effects.
Remove 3D volume cues from the camera bodies to match the 2D appearance.
Reconsider the use of animation/steps to focus on transitions that match textbook intent.
actionItems: Redesign arrows so there is a single thick red arrow segment between each camera as in the source (not passing through cameras).

attempts.html:
Show guide labels
Forward camera translation
Flat side-view sketch: each red arrow points to the next camera position.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The generated figure arranges the cameras and arrows diagonally, whereas the original arranges them horizontally in a straight line.
Figure includes UI controls and optional labels not found in the original.
Rendered camera sketches are faithful but slightly stylistically different (subtle shading, extra lines).
Some screen labels (when enabled) overlap and clutter the figure, which is not an issue in the original.
End arrows in the generated figure may not precisely match the thickness and arrowhead style from the original.
evaluation.notes: Functionally and conceptually faithful with excellent interactivity, but key composition differs (diagonal arrangement violates the straight horizontal layout of the source); minor labeling and stylistic discrepancies.
action_items: Reposition the entire sequence so all camera/arrow snapshots are perfectly horizontally aligned, matching the source figure's flat composition.
Refine camera icon and arrow style to even more closely match the stark schematic look of the original (reduce shading, thicken arrow shafts, sharpen heads as needed).
Improve label placement or default to labels off to avoid screen clutter and maintain clean textbook look.
Optionally, simplify/remove UI text descriptions for pure visual fidelity mode.

feedback.rationale: The action items focus on layout corrections, style tweaks, and label/UI adjustments—all implementation details—while the plan's conceptual insight and decomposition were accurate.
action_items: Reposition the entire sequence so all camera/arrow snapshots are perfectly horizontally aligned, matching the source figure's flat composition.
Refine camera icon and arrow style to even more closely match the stark schematic look of the original (reduce shading, thicken arrow shafts, sharpen heads as needed).
Improve label placement or default to labels off to avoid screen clutter and maintain clean textbook look.
Optionally, simplify/remove UI text descriptions for pure visual fidelity mode.

attempts.html:
Horizontal camera translation
All camera centers lie on one straight horizontal line; each red arrow points to the next position.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Optional guide labels are present when toggled, but the original has no labels.
Camera icons are consistent and plausible, but not pixel-for-pixel identical to the textbook style (minor stroke width/angle variations).
Arrowhead style and thickness are close but somewhat stylized compared to the original; original arrows are simpler and sharper.
Arrows and cameras are precisely aligned horizontally, as in the source figure.
Spacing and quantity of cameras/arrows are adjustable, while the original has fixed elements only.
evaluation.notes: Faithfully captures the core layout and relationships, offers excellent, non-invasive interactions; minor artistic stylization vs. original.
action_items: Slightly sharpen arrowhead profiles to more closely match textbook's straight-edged style.
Decrease optional guide label default opacity to avoid accidental visibility mismatch with the label toggle.
Consider slight adjustment to camera icon side angles for perfect visual mimicry of the original.
Make sure z-ordering always puts black outlines on top of all gray if any overlap arises.
Maintain default with guide labels off to match original's label-free appearance.

feedback.rationale: All required elements and interactions are present and correct, but minor implementation refinements are needed in arrow styling, label opacity, icon angles, and rendering order.
